2026-07-24 08:46:25

Market Last Trading Day - U.S. equities weakened during the last trading day as investors continued to evaluate earnings, economic data, and the outlook for interest rates. The decline was sufficient to confirm a transition from RiskOn to RiskOff, prompting TurboRegime to execute a portfolio rebalance near the market close. Consistent with the strategy's end-of-day methodology, the trade was executed only after the signal was confirmed, avoiding the intraday head fakes that can occur during periods of elevated market volatility.

Market Today - TurboRegime begins today operating under the newly confirmed RiskOff regime. The portfolio has already been rebalanced to the recommended defensive allocation, so no additional action is currently required. While markets will continue responding to earnings releases and economic news, the strategy will remain focused on objective end-of-day market conditions rather than attempting to predict short-term price movements. The current allocation will remain in place until market conditions once again support increasing portfolio risk.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOff is now the active regime. The current portfolio allocation is 60% SPY, 40% SHV, and 0% SPXL. Although the strategy has become more defensive, it remains substantially invested in the equity market. The objective is not to exit stocks, but to reduce portfolio risk during periods when market conditions become less favorable. TurboRegime will continue evaluating the TReg Regime Signal each trading day and will increase equity exposure only after a confirmed improvement in market conditions.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. 

Live Performance - TurboRegime continues to substantially outperform a buy-and-hold investment in the S&P 500 since live tracking began on April 1 while remaining closely aligned with historical backtest performance. Since mid-May, however, the market has been characterized by repeated reversals and limited follow-through rather than sustained trends. This has resulted in several regime changes over a relatively short period. During these choppy market conditions, the strategy has adapted by shifting between RiskOn and RiskOff as market conditions evolved. While some of these recent trades have produced modest gains or losses, they have generally outperformed a fully invested S&P 500 portfolio over the same periods because TurboRegime maintains allocations to short-term Treasury securities during both RiskOn and RiskOff regimes. The objective is not to avoid every losing trade, but to consistently manage downside risk while remaining positioned to participate when stronger market trends emerge.

General Commentary - Markets spend far more time transitioning between trends than moving in a straight line. Those transition periods often produce uncertainty, conflicting headlines, and frequent reversals that challenge investors to remain disciplined. TurboRegime is designed specifically for these environments. Rather than attempting to forecast where the market is headed next, the strategy responds only after market conditions have objectively changed. This disciplined, rules-based approach helps remove emotion from the investment process and allows long-term performance to be driven by consistent execution rather than short-term predictions.
